creator: dev@submerge.ch
text:
Thanks for reporting this. Somewhat strange that nobody else complained about it yet. We had to disable VST3 by default because audio/vst3sdk was broken for some people. That may be fixed now. I use Ardour with VST3 enabled locally, I will test with VST3 disabled tomorrow.

It's well possible that the "--no-vst3" configure flag doesn't properly disable all VST3 related code in the Ardour build. That flag changed semantics back and forth during the 8.x releases.

When you have the results of your build with VST3 enabled, could you also comment on your environment like FreeBSD release, architecture etc.?
